name: Kobo Libra Colour
introText: The Kobo Libra Colour is a 7-inch color E-ink e-reader with stylus support, OverDrive library borrowing, and open ePub format support — the best e-reader for Canadians who use public library e-books.
whatWorks: Native OverDrive/Libby library support — borrow free e-books from Canadian public libraries directly on the device, without sideloading or workarounds that Kindle requires.
Open ePub support — the Kobo accepts ePub files from any source including Project Gutenberg and Smashwords, without Amazon's DRM restrictions.
worthKnowing: 6-week battery is shorter than Kindle Paperwhite (12 weeks) — color E-ink draws more power. Still excellent compared to any tablet.
Kobo's book store is smaller than Amazon Kindle — major releases are available, but book selection for niche topics and older titles can be more limited.

commonComplaints.title: Smaller book store than Amazon
commonComplaints.body: Kobo's store has millions of titles but Amazon Kindle has more, especially for self-published and independent titles.

commonComplaints.title: Battery shorter than Paperwhite
commonComplaints.body: The Libra Colour's 6-week battery vs. Paperwhite's 12 weeks means charging twice as often. Still excellent but a notable difference.

commonComplaints.title: Interface less polished than Kindle
commonComplaints.body: Kobo's software is good but Kindle's interface is more refined and faster in some navigational tasks. Subjective but consistent feedback.

verdict: The Kobo Libra Colour is the best e-reader for Canadians who borrow library e-books through Libby or OverDrive — native library support is the killer feature that Kindle doesn't match. At $219, it's also cheaper than the Kindle Colorsoft. If you buy from Amazon, the Kindle ecosystem is better; if you use a public library, Kobo wins.

realBuyers.quote: Library card plus Kobo = free books forever. Borrowed 200 books without spending a dollar on the book store.
Used a Kindle for 5 years. Switched to Kobo for the library support. Never going back.
Color display is great for illustrated books. My kids' books actually look good on it.

sources.title: Kobo Libra Colour Review — The Verge

metaDescription: Kobo Libra Colour review Canada: 7" color E-ink, OverDrive library support, IPX8. $219 CAD. Best e-reader for Canadians with a library card.
